title: Diocese of Antipolo Opens Jubilee for Our Lady of
content:
News | Diocese of Antipolo Opens Jubilee for Our Lady of Antipolo

A historic milestone unfolded last January 15, 2026, at Antipolo Cathedral as the Diocese of Antipolo opened the 400th year of devotion and 100th canonical anniversary of Our Lady of Antipolo. Media teams from television and radio stations across the Philippines and students from MAPSA Antipolo schools attended the ceremony, joining in the celebration of faith and service.

Bishop Ruperto Cruz Santos, D.D., welcomed the attendees, highlighting the jubilee theme Paglalakbay, which portrays faith as a journey, pag-aakay as Mary guiding the faithful to Jesus, and pag-aalay as offering one’s life in service, love, and generosity. He called the event a momentous year for the diocese and a reminder of God’s guidance through the intercession of Our Lady of Antipolo.

Fr. Glenn William Relucio, overall coordinator, said the jubilee aims to share the Filipino faith journey with present and future generations. Organizers also presented the yearlong activities, including six Jubilee Churches, symbolic roads representing faith, peace, hope, and love, and pilgrimage items such as the Jubilee Passport and souvenir tokens.

Activities will take place within and beyond the diocese, from Jubilee Journey Doors to Marian pilgrimages, visits to Quiapo, a fluvial parade, and the arrival of the Cebu image in July. Church leaders encouraged youth participation through schools and social media, reminding all that devotion to Our Lady of Antipolo continues to guide and renew hearts as the new year begins.

: Maria Jhenelle T. Hilario & Angelica Jemiel B. Jatulan

: Angelica Jemiel B. Jatulan
